title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a student called Sarah Turner talking about her experience working on an urban gardening project. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a student called Sarah Turner talking about her experience working on an urban gardening project. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: Sarah Turner talks about her experience working on an urban gardening project.
extracts.audio_script: Hello, I’m Sarah Turner, and I’d like to share my experience of participating in an urban gardening project. It all started when I joined a community initiative focused on improving our local environment by creating green spaces. The inspiration came when I was [7]attending a workshop[/7] on sustainability during my university years. It drew me to the idea of transforming unused urban areas into beautiful gardens. Our first project was in a run-down neighborhood, where we transformed an abandoned lot. Initially, it was challenging due to [8]poor soil condition[/8], but with the help of a local gardener, we learned techniques to enhance the soil quality and support plant growth. As the project progressed, I realized the importance of community involvement, and we organized several [9]workshops[/9] for residents to encourage their participation. Over time, the garden became not just a green space but a hub for socializing and relaxation. If there’s one thing I’ve learned, it's the power of collaboration in achieving common goals. Our project thrived due to consistent [10]community support[/10], and I was amazed by how much we could accomplish together. We also ensured that our project was sustainable by focusing on [11]using native plants[/11], which required less maintenance and promoted local biodiversity. Reflecting on this journey, I am proud of how we converted a neglected area into a vital part of our community. I hope to inspire others to start similar initiatives and contribute to an eco-friendly urban landscape. Now, I’m pursuing a career in urban planning, with a focus on sustainable development. This path was heavily influenced by my time spent on the gardening project. I believe that with proper planning, urban spaces can be transformed to benefit both people and the environment, creating a symbiotic relationship. Thank you for listening to my story.

dialogue.speaker: sarah
dialogue.text: Hello, I’m Sarah Turner, and I’d like to share my experience of participating in an urban gardening project. It all started when I joined a community initiative focused on improving our local environment by creating green spaces. The inspiration came when I was attending a workshop on sustainability during my university years. It drew me to the idea of transforming unused urban areas into beautiful gardens. Our first project was in a run-down neighborhood, where we transformed an abandoned lot. Initially, it was challenging due to poor soil condition, but with the help of a local gardener, we learned techniques to enhance the soil quality and support plant growth. As the project progressed, I realized the importance of community involvement, and we organized several workshops for residents to encourage their participation. Over time, the garden became not just a green space but a hub for socializing and relaxation. If there’s one thing I’ve learned, it's the power of collaboration in achieving common goals. Our project thrived due to consistent community support, and I was amazed by how much we could accomplish together. We also ensured that our project was sustainable by focusing on using native plants, which required less maintenance and promoted local biodiversity. Reflecting on this journey, I am proud of how we converted a neglected area into a vital part of our community. I hope to inspire others to start similar initiatives and contribute to an eco-friendly urban landscape. Now, I’m pursuing a career in urban planning, with a focus on sustainable development. This path was heavily influenced by my time spent on the gardening project. I believe that with proper planning, urban spaces can be transformed to benefit both people and the environment, creating a symbiotic relationship. Thank you for listening to my story.
